UserProfile.title: UserProfile
UserProfile.description: Represents a user's profile within the MediMate application.

id.description: Unique identifier for the UserProfile entity.

profilePicture.description: URL of the user's profile picture.

onboardingCompleted.description: Indicates if the user has completed the initial onboarding flow.

Doctor.title: Doctor
Doctor.description: Represents a doctor available for consultation.

id.description: Unique identifier for the Doctor entity.

Appointment.title: Appointment
Appointment.description: Represents a scheduled appointment between a user and a doctor.

id.description: Unique identifier for the Appointment entity.

dateTime.description: The date and time of the appointment.

reminderSet.description: Indicates whether a reminder is set for the appointment.

Medicine.title: Medicine
Medicine.description: Represents a medicine available in the medical store.

id.description: Unique identifier for the Medicine entity.

description.description: A brief description of the medicine.

MedicalOrder.title: MedicalOrder
MedicalOrder.description: Represents an order for medicine placed by a user.

id.description: Unique identifier for the MedicalOrder entity.

orderDate.description: The date the order was placed.

totalAmount.description: The total amount of the order.

Auth.title: Auth
Auth.description: Represents authentication information for a user. Note: This entity does not store sensitive information such as passwords. Authentication is assumed to be handled by an external system.

id.description: Unique identifier for the Auth entity.

userProfileId.description: Reference to UserProfile. (Relationship: Auth 1:1 UserProfile). This should be the same as UserProfile.id for a 1:1 relationship.

providerUserId.description: The user ID provided by the authentication provider.

definition.description: Stores user profile data. Each user has their own profile document. 'userId' and 'userProfileId' will be same for a 1:1 relationship.

params.name: userId
params.description: The unique identifier for the user.

params.name: userProfileId
params.description: The unique identifier for the user profile.

definition.description: Stores appointment data for each user. 'userId' is the owner.

params.name: userId
params.description: The unique identifier for the user.

params.name: appointmentId
params.description: The unique identifier for the appointment.

definition.description: Stores medical order data for each user. 'userId' is the owner.

params.name: userId
params.description: The unique identifier for the user.

params.name: medicalOrderId
params.description: The unique identifier for the medical order.

params.name: doctorId
params.description: The unique identifier for the doctor.

params.name: medicineId
params.description: The unique identifier for the medicine.

definition.description: Stores authentication data. 'userId' is the authentication id.

params.name: userId
params.description: The unique identifier for the auth.

firestore.reasoning: The Firestore data structure is designed to ensure authorization independence, clarity, and scalability. User profiles and their related data (appointments, medical orders) are stored under the `/users/{userId}` path, enabling path-based ownership for private data. The `Auth` entity is placed in a separate `/auth/{userId}` collection. Doctors and Medicines are placed in root-level collections.  This structure avoids hierarchical authorization dependencies, supports atomic operations, and simplifies security rules. Appointments and MedicalOrders are stored as subcollections under the user's document, ensuring clear ownership and straightforward security rules based on the user's ID.
